response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack developer with broad polyglot experience, but the resume shows minimal evidence of deep Java/Kotlin/JVM work or the Spring ecosystem required for this role. Most recent experience centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with Java appearing only briefly in older roles (Altom, robotic arms) and no demonstrated experience with IDE internals, language tooling, or enterprise JVM frameworks like Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate.
strengths: Strong systems and tooling background, including C/C++ work on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments
Proven track record on complex, large-scale codebases and low-latency microservices
Solid software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testing libraries, trunk-based development)
concerns: No demonstrated Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate or enterprise Java framework experience
Kotlin not mentioned anywhere in the resume
Java listed as a skill but appears only peripherally in older roles, with no recent JVM-focused work

key_factors.factor: Prior IDE/C-IDE component experience at National Instruments
Recent stack focused on TypeScript/Node rather than JVM
